preFix.title: Burn-relief salve
preFix.subtitle: A lavender and aloe vera salve for minor burns, sunburn, and heat-reddened skin
preFix.excerpt: Aloe vera gel cools the skin on contact; lavender essential oil has documented wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties at the concentrations used here; beeswax and coconut oil form a protective occlusive barrier over the affected area.
preFix.sourceNotes: Lavender oil wound healing: Mori et al., Phytomedicine (2016). Aloe vera on minor burns and sunburn: Dat et al., Cochrane Database (2012). Occlusive moisturisation for skin barrier repair: standard dermatology reference.

content.text: This salve is for minor burns, sunburn, and heat-reddened skin.
cools the skin on contact; lavender essential oil at 1% is calming and wound-supportive; beeswax and coconut oil form a protective layer over the skin.

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: about 1 ml; approximately 1.5% of total batch

content.text: Melt the beeswax pellets and coconut oil together in a double boiler over a gentle heat. Stir until fully melted. Remove from the heat and allow to cool slightly to 50 to 55°C.

Stir in the aloe vera gel -- the mixture may look temporarily grainy as the gel meets the oil phase; keep stirring. Add the lavender essential oil and stir through. Work quickly as the beeswax will begin to firm up.
Pour into two salve tins immediately. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least 30 minutes to set. The finished salve will be a pale, firm balm.

For minor burns: cool the area under cold running water for at least 10 minutes first, then apply a thin layer of salve and leave uncovered. For sunburn: apply liberally to affected areas, repeat as needed. Do not apply to open wounds, broken skin, or severe burns (blistering, deep reddening) -- seek medical attention instead.
Keep at room temperature in the sealed tin. Use within one year. Discard if the salve develops a rancid or stale smell.

initialFindings.message: body contains medical-disclaimer-style text but does NOT include the locked phrase "Not medical advice. Consult a medical professional for ongoing or serious symptoms." — normalise
